title: Luca and the Missing Rainbow
synopsis: When the rainbow disappears from the sky after a storm, young scientist Luca teams up with his diverse group of friends to solve the mystery using observation and scientific thinking.

pages.text: Luca loved rainy days. He loved watching the drops race down his window. He loved the smell of wet grass. But most of all, he loved rainbows! Today it had rained hard. The sun was coming out. "Time for a rainbow!" said Luca. He ran outside to look. But where was the rainbow? The sky was empty! "Mom!" called Luca. "The rainbow is missing!"

illustration.description: A young Latino boy with dark curly hair and big expressive eyes looks up at a cloudy but brightening sky with concern. He's standing in a puddle-filled backyard with green grass and colorful flowers. The lighting shows post-rain sunshine breaking through gray clouds.
illustration.alt: Luca looking up at the sky searching for a missing rainbow

pages.text: Mom came outside. She had dark hair like Luca and wore a yellow apron. "What do you mean, missing?" she asked. "There should be a rainbow right now!" said Luca. "The sun is out. It just rained. But I don't see one anywhere!" Mom looked at the sky too. "That is strange," she said. "Maybe we need to investigate." Luca's eyes lit up. He loved mysteries!

illustration.description: A kind Latina woman in a bright yellow apron stands next to Luca, both looking up at the sky with puzzled expressions. The backyard setting shows wet pavement reflecting sunlight, with a wooden fence and flowering bushes in rich greens and purples.
illustration.alt: Luca and his mom looking puzzled at the empty sky

pages.text: "I need my detective kit!" said Luca. He ran inside and got his backpack. It had a magnifying glass, a notebook, and colored pencils. "And I need my science friends!" Luca called his friend Zara next door. Then he called his friend Jin down the street. "Meet me at the park," he said. "We have a rainbow mystery to solve!"

illustration.description: Luca excitedly packs his bright blue backpack with detective supplies on his bedroom floor. His room has science posters on the walls, toy dinosaurs, and books scattered around. Warm golden light streams through the window, creating a cozy, adventurous mood.
illustration.alt: Luca packing his detective kit in his colorful bedroom

pages.text: At the park, Luca met his friends. Zara had beautiful dark skin and wore purple glasses. She loved plants and bugs. Jin had straight black hair and always carried a calculator. He loved numbers and patterns. "So what's this mystery?" asked Zara. "The rainbow is gone!" said Luca. "It should be here, but it's not!" "Hmm," said Jin. "Let's think like scientists."

illustration.description: Three diverse children meet in a sunny park with green grass and tall oak trees. Zara, a Black girl with purple glasses and a bright green t-shirt, stands next to Jin, an Asian boy with neat black hair holding a small calculator. Luca gestures excitedly between them. The scene is warm and friendly.
illustration.alt: Luca meeting his friends Zara and Jin at the park

pages.text: "What makes rainbows?" asked Zara. Luca opened his notebook. "Sunlight and water drops," he said. "The light bounces inside the water drops and makes colors." "So we need both sun AND water," said Jin. He looked around. "I see sun. But where are the water drops?" The three friends looked up at the sky. The clouds were moving away fast! "The water drops are leaving!" said Zara.

illustration.description: The three children look up at a dynamic sky with white clouds moving rapidly across bright blue. Jin points upward while Zara adjusts her purple glasses. Luca holds his open notebook with colorful drawings of rainbows and water drops. The scene captures movement and discovery.
illustration.alt: The friends observing the moving clouds in the sky

pages.text: "Wait!" said Luca. He had an idea. "What if we make our own water drops?" "How?" asked his friends. Luca grinned. "The park fountain!" They ran to the big fountain in the center of the park. Water sprayed high in the air. Tiny drops sparkled in the sunlight. "Perfect!" said Jin. "Now we just need to find the right spot to see the rainbow!"

illustration.description: A beautiful stone fountain shoots water high into the air, creating sparkling droplets that catch the golden sunlight. The three excited children run toward it across emerald green grass, with park benches and blooming flower beds visible. The water droplets shimmer like tiny diamonds.
illustration.alt: The children running toward a sparkling fountain

pages.text: The friends walked around the fountain. They looked from every side. "I don't see anything," said Zara. "Me neither," said Jin. "Wait," said Luca. "My mom taught me something. The rainbow appears opposite the sun." He pointed. "The sun is over there. So the rainbow should be... over there!" They moved to the other side of the fountain.

illustration.description: The three children stand on different sides of the ornate fountain, looking carefully at the water spray. Luca points decisively in one direction while Zara and Jin follow his gesture. The afternoon sun creates long golden rays across the park scene, with purple and pink flowers blooming nearby.
illustration.alt: Luca pointing out the direction opposite the sun at the fountain

pages.text: "There it is!" shouted Zara. A beautiful rainbow curved through the fountain spray! All the colors were there - red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple. "We found it!" cheered Jin. "We solved the mystery!" said Luca. He drew the rainbow in his notebook. Other kids came running over. "Look!" called a girl with red braids. "Look!" called a boy in a wheelchair. "It's so pretty!"

illustration.description: A brilliant rainbow arcs through the fountain spray with all colors vividly displayed. The three main characters cheer with arms raised in victory. In the background, other diverse children including a red-haired girl and a boy in a bright blue wheelchair approach with wonder and excitement.
illustration.alt: Children celebrating the discovery of the rainbow in the fountain

pages.text: Soon many kids were watching the fountain rainbow. Everyone was smiling and laughing. "You know what?" said Zara. "This rainbow is even better than a sky rainbow." "Why?" asked Jin. "Because we made it ourselves!" said Luca proudly. "We used science to solve the mystery." The three friends high-fived. They had turned a missing rainbow into an even more amazing discovery! "Best mystery ever," said Luca.

illustration.description: A joyful group of diverse children of various ethnicities gather around the fountain, all pointing and smiling at the rainbow. The scene is filled with warm golden light, vibrant rainbow colors, and expressions of pure wonder and happiness. The fountain creates a magical centerpiece with sparkling water drops everywhere.
illustration.alt: A diverse group of happy children enjoying the fountain rainbow together

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What two things do you need to make a rainbow?
questions.explanation: Rainbows form when sunlight passes through water drops in the air. The light bends and separates into different colors.

questions.question: Where should you look to see a rainbow?
questions.explanation: Rainbows always appear in the part of the sky opposite from where the sun is shining.

questions.question: Why couldn't Luca see the rainbow in the sky?
questions.explanation: The clouds with water drops moved away quickly after the rain, so there were no water drops left in the sky to make a rainbow.

questions.question: How did the friends solve the rainbow mystery?
questions.explanation: The friends used the park fountain to create their own water drops in the air, which made a rainbow with the sunlight.

questions.question: What did Luca use to investigate the mystery?
questions.explanation: Luca used his detective kit which had a magnifying glass, notebook, and colored pencils to help solve the mystery.

pages.title: Rainbow Science Fill-in
pages.instructions: Fill in the blanks to complete the rainbow science facts!
pages.text: Rainbows need two things: _____ and water drops. The light bounces inside the water drops and makes _____. You can see a rainbow _____ the sun. Luca and his friends made their own rainbow using a _____.

pages.title: Mystery Detective Fill-in
pages.instructions: Help Luca solve the mystery by filling in the missing words!
pages.text: Luca packed his _____ with detective supplies. He called his friends _____ and Jin to help solve the mystery. They looked at the _____ and noticed the water drops were moving away. The friends worked together as _____ to find the answer.
